title: Ord Vaug

{{Quote|You're picking Lilli and Endro up at an internment facility we just liberated on Ord Vaug. Colonel Onassi will fill you in on the rest and give you the access coordinates for a safe approach to the planet.|[[Lando Calrissian/Legends|Lando Calrissian]], to Han Solo|<ref name="VEA">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20040304vandelhelm|text=Vandelhelm: Enemies and Alloys|int=Vandelhelm: Enemies and Alloys}}</ref>}}
'''Ord Vaug''' was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]] in the [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region]]'s [[Rocantor sector/Legends|Rocantor sector]]. The site of a major [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] propaganda [[Battle of Ord Vaug|victory]] over [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatist]] [[Separatist holdouts/Legends|holdouts]] in [[17 BBY/Legends|17 BBY]], Ord Vaug was the site of an Imperial [[Prison/Legends|prison]] that at one point held the [[Venerated Ones]] of [[Vandelhelm/Legends|Vandelhelm]]. Ord Vaug was [[Liberation of Ord Vaug|liberated]] by the [[Alliance of Free Planets]] in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], and the Venerated Ones returned home.

==Description==
The [[Planet/Legends|planet]] Ord Vaug was located in the [[Ord Vaug system/Legends|Ord Vaug system]] of the [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region]]'s [[Rocantor sector/Legends|Rocantor sector]].<ref name="EAOC" /> Ord Vaug's natural environment was marked by rainforests, and had an [[Atmosphere/Legends|atmosphere]] easily breathable for [[Human/Legends|Humans]].<ref name="SW98" />

==History==
Ord Vaug had origins as one of the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]]'s [[Ordnance/Regional Depot/Legends|Ordnance/Regional Depots]] during the [[Pius Dea Era]], specifically during the [[Fifteenth Pius Dea Crusade]] centered on nearby [[Old Barag/Legends|Old Barag]] in [[11,591 BBY]]. Many millennia later, Ord Vaug became the site of a highly publicized [[Battle of Ord Vaug|battle]] during the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Western Reaches Operation]] against [[Confederacy of Independent Systems/Legends|Separatist]] [[Separatist holdouts/Legends|holdouts]] in [[17 BBY/Legends|17 BBY]], where [[General/Legends|General]] [[Hurst Romodi/Legends|Hurst Romodi]] secured a major victory in front of [[HoloNet News/Legends|HoloNet News]] crews—the battle on Ord Vaug was later adapted into a [[Holodrama/Legends|holodrama]].<ref name="TEGtW" /> 

During the [[Imperial Era/Legends|Imperial Period]], Ord Vaug was the site of an Imperial [[Prison/Legends|prison]] that held foreign dignitaries, including the young [[Venerated Ones]] of the planet [[Vandelhelm/Legends|Vandelhelm]]. The two children, [[Lillindri Nanimei Filda Vandelhelm XXXII|Lillindri "Lilli" Nanimei Filda Vandelhelm XXXII]] and her younger brother [[Endrodanar Tiopuld Shaamador Vandelhelm XXIV|Endrodanar "Endro" Tiopuld Shaamador Vandelhelm XXIV]], were confined on Ord Vaug for three years as part of a scheme to exert Imperial control over Vandelhelm's valuable metalsmithing industry. The [[Alliance of Free Planets]] [[Liberation of Ord Vaug|liberated]] Ord Vaug in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], freeing the prisoners—afterwards, Alliance heroes [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and [[Nien Nunb/Legends|Nien Nunb]] were dispatched to Ord Vaug in order to bring the Venerated Ones home.<ref name="SW98" />

==Behind the scenes==
Ord Vaug briefly appeared in [[Star Wars (1977) 98|''Star Wars'' (1977) 98]], written by [[Archie Goodwin]], illustrated by [[Al Williamson]] and released in [[1985]].<ref name="SW98" /> Although initially only identified as an Imperial prison planet, Ord Vaug was later named in the [[2004]] [[Wizards of the Coast]] supplement ''[[Vandelhelm: Enemies and Alloys]]'', written by [[Cory J. Herndon]].<ref name="VEA" />
